headers.comment: Baseline security headers. Only HSTS was being sent. No CSP here — this site inlines styles and scripts throughout, so a policy strict enough to be worth having would need a nonce pass first; a permissive one is theatre.

Static media in /public carried max-age=0, must-revalidate — the Vercel default when nothing is declared. Every repeat visit paid a measured 0.30-0.39s revalidation round trip, including on the LCP image. /_astro/* already gets immutable from Astro's content hashing; these files are not hashed, so they get a day of freshness plus a week of stale-while-revalidate rather than immutable — replacing bg-tile.webp must not strand viewers on the old bytes for a year.

A month, NOT immutable. These filenames encode weight (switzer-900.woff2, pierson-thin.ttf), not a content hash, so the same URL can legitimately serve different bytes — and global.css:18-22 documents swapping the display face for a licensed one as planned work. immutable suppresses revalidation even on a manual reload, which would strand returning visitors on the old face for up to a year with no recovery short of renaming the file.
